title: Sarah Paine - Why Putin and Xi can't escape geography
summary: Military historian Sarah Paine presents a two-thousand-year geopolitical framework distinguishing continental empires pursuing security through territorial conquest (China, Russia) from maritime powers pursuing prosperity through trade (Britain, US). The lecture explains how geography shapes great power strategy and traces Industrial Revolution impacts on this divide.
writeup: Military historian Sarah Paine lays out a two-thousand-year framework that separates continental empires, which seek security through territorial conquest, from maritime powers, which seek prosperity through trade. She places China and Russia in the continental camp and Britain and the US in the maritime one, arguing that geography shapes the strategy each great power adopts. The lecture also traces how the Industrial Revolution reshaped this continental versus maritime divide.
